questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh holds the distinction of being the first Indian Governor of which institution?
questions.explanation: Deshmukh was the first Indian to serve as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India.

questions.q: Which Union ministerial portfolio did C.D. Deshmukh hold after his central-banking career?
questions.explanation: He later served as the Union Finance Minister of India.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh is best described as a:
questions.explanation: He is remembered as an economist statesman who shaped India's early economic institutions.

questions.q: The Reserve Bank of India, which Deshmukh led, is the country's:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India is India's central bank, and Deshmukh was its first Indian Governor.

questions.q: What do the initials C.D. in C.D. Deshmukh stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Chintaman Dwarakanath Deshmukh.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh played a key role in shaping India's:
questions.explanation: He played a central role in building India's early economic and financial institutions.

questions.q: As Finance Minister, Deshmukh was responsible for presenting which document to Parliament?
questions.explanation: As Finance Minister he presented the Union Budget of India to Parliament.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh served as Finance Minister in the cabinet of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Finance Minister in the cabinet of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was C.D. Deshmukh born?
questions.explanation: He was born in the region of present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Deshmukh became the first Indian Governor of the RBI, succeeding governors who were previously of which background?
questions.explanation: He was the first Indian to hold the post, which had previously been held by British governors.

questions.q: In which year did C.D. Deshmukh become the first Indian Governor of the Reserve Bank of India?
questions.explanation: Deshmukh became the first Indian Governor of the RBI in 1943.

questions.q: In which year was C.D. Deshmukh born?
questions.explanation: Chintaman Dwarakanath Deshmukh was born on 14 January 1896.

questions.q: Before joining the Reserve Bank, Deshmukh was a member of which elite administrative service?
questions.explanation: He had joined the Indian Civil Service before moving into central banking.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh became Union Finance Minister in which year?
questions.explanation: He joined the Union cabinet as Finance Minister in 1950.

questions.q: Deshmukh's tenure as RBI Governor ended in which year, when he moved on to other roles?
questions.explanation: He served as RBI Governor until 1949.

questions.q: As Finance Minister, Deshmukh oversaw the early years of which national planning effort?
questions.explanation: As Finance Minister he was closely associated with the early Five-Year Plans of India.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh was a member of the Planning Commission in addition to being Finance Minister. The Planning Commission was set up in which year?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission, of which he was an ex-officio member, was set up in 1950.

questions.q: In which year did C.D. Deshmukh pass away?
questions.explanation: C.D. Deshmukh died on 2 October 1982.

questions.q: After leaving government, Deshmukh served as the head of which premier academic body?
questions.explanation: He later served as Chairman of the University Grants Commission.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh was conferred which national civilian honour in recognition of his public service?
questions.explanation: He was honoured with the Padma Vibhushan for his contributions to public life.

questions.q: Deshmukh was associated with the founding of which institute of social and economic research in Delhi?
options: The All India Institute of Medical Sciences
questions.explanation: He was closely associated with the establishment of the India International Centre in Delhi.

questions.q: The currency notes issued by the RBI during Deshmukh's tenure as the first Indian Governor would carry the signature of:
questions.explanation: RBI-issued currency notes carry the signature of the Governor, a role he held as the first Indian to do so.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh served as Union Finance Minister until which year, when he left the cabinet?
questions.explanation: He served as Finance Minister until 1956, when he resigned from the cabinet.

questions.q: Deshmukh topped the Indian Civil Service examination in his year; he had earlier studied at which university abroad?
questions.explanation: He studied at the University of Cambridge before entering the Indian Civil Service.

questions.q: As RBI Governor, Deshmukh helped guide the central bank through which major wartime and transitional period?
options: The Second World War and the run-up to independence
questions.explanation: His RBI governorship from 1943 to 1949 spanned the Second World War and the transition to independence.

questions.q: C.D. Deshmukh represented India at which international financial conference associated with founding the IMF and World Bank?
questions.explanation: He was part of India's delegation to the Bretton Woods Conference that shaped the post-war financial order.

questions.q: Deshmukh's resignation from the Union cabinet in 1956 occurred amid debate over which issue?
options: The reorganisation of states and status of Bombay city
questions.explanation: His 1956 resignation came in the context of the states reorganisation debate over the status of Bombay city.

questions.q: Deshmukh was the RBI Governor when the bank was nationalised. The RBI was nationalised in which year?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was nationalised in 1949, during the latter part of Deshmukh's governorship.

questions.q: Deshmukh authored an autobiography reflecting on his public career, titled:
questions.explanation: His autobiography is titled 'The Course of My Life.'

questions.q: Deshmukh chaired the University Grants Commission for a period spanning which decade?
questions.explanation: He led the University Grants Commission from the mid-1950s into the early 1960s.
